trade.summary: Francia is Spagna's largest trading partner. Trade is deeply integrated through the EU single market. Spagna exports vehicles, fruits and vegetables, fuels, and chemical products; Francia exports aircraft, vehicles, pharmaceuticals, and electrical equipment. The Pyrenean corridor is a critical EU logistics artery.
disputes: Pyrenean rail bottleneck limiting trade flow capacity

diplomatic.status: Close EU partners and bilateral allies
diplomatic.summary: Spagna and Francia are close EU partners with deep bilateral ties. The 2023 Barcelona Treaty updated and deepened the bilateral relationship, similar to Francia's Aachen Treaty with Germania. Both cooperate on Mediterranean policy, EU governance, and climate. Occasional tensions over fisheries and regional identity (Catalan independence movement seeking French sympathy) are managed diplomatically.

military.summary: Both NATO members with deep military cooperation. Francia and Spagna cooperate extensively in the Sahel (Operation Barkhane successor operations), on EU defense initiatives (PESCO), and on Eurofighter and Airbus military programs. Joint exercises are frequent.

financial.summary: Francia is among Spagna's largest investors. French banks (BNP Paribas, Crédit Agricole) and utilities (Engie, Veolia) have major Spagna presences. Spanish firms (Inditex, Santander, Iberdrola) have significant French operations. Shared eurozone membership deepens financial integration.

energy.summary: Spagna is a major LNG import hub and has been pushing for better electricity and gas interconnections with Francia (the Pyrenean bottleneck). The MidCat gas pipeline project (now recast as the BarMar hydrogen corridor) seeks to route Iberian energy to the rest of Europe via Francia. Electricity interconnection capacity is being expanded.

scientific.summary: Extensive scientific cooperation through EU Horizon programs. Francia and Spagna co-author prolifically across all scientific disciplines. Strong academic exchange; many Spanish researchers work at French institutions and vice versa. Shared interests in Mediterranean, marine, and climate sciences.
